old_value: {"id":"flotsync-8hn","title":"Review snapshot datamodel + InMemoryData serialization flow","description":"Review the new schema/datamodel snapshot shape and InMemoryData::visit_row_snapshot integration, including ID-shape split (LVW uses base Id; linear history uses IdWithIndex\u003cId\u003e) and test coverage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T16:13:28Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-02-25T16:13:28Z","labels":["area:data-types-schema"]}

{"id":"flotsync-s02","title":"Review operations datamodel shape and field-level API","description":"After snapshot review approval, review schema/datamodel operations API shape and confirm it is final enough to lock serialization and tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T16:13:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-02-25T16:13:29Z","labels":["area:data-types-schema"]}

new_value: {"description":"Add reusable in-memory orchestration for constructing full-row operations (partial field updates) so tests and transports do not hand-assemble per-field operations.","title":"Add InMemoryData helper API to create row operations"}
old_value: {"id":"flotsync-6vh","title":"Add InMemoryData helper to drive row operation serialization","description":"Add reusable in-memory orchestration for full-row operation emission (partial field updates) so tests and transports do not hand-drive per-field visitor flow.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-02-25T16:13:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-02-25T16:13:29Z","labels":["area:data-types-schema"]}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.4","title":"Add protobuf schema and UpdateId wire access for datamodel transport","description":"Define protobuf messages for the schema snapshot and schema operation surface specialized to UpdateId, and add the minimal flotsync_core API needed for flotsync_messages to construct and inspect UpdateId during wire conversion.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Protobuf messages cover dataset snapshots, row snapshots, history node streams, state field payloads, and schema operations using UpdateId. 2) flotsync_core exposes the minimal public API needed to encode and decode UpdateId outside the crate. 3) The message layout is documented well enough for the codec tasks to implement against it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","labels":["area:messages-protobuf"]}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.4","title":"Add protobuf schema and UpdateId wire access for datamodel transport","description":"Define protobuf messages for the schema snapshot and schema operation surface specialized to UpdateId, and add the minimal flotsync_core API needed for flotsync_messages to construct and inspect UpdateId during wire conversion.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Protobuf messages cover dataset snapshots, row snapshots, history node streams, state field payloads, and schema operations using UpdateId. 2) flotsync_core exposes the minimal public API needed to encode and decode UpdateId outside the crate. 3) The message layout is documented well enough for the codec tasks to implement against it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T18:21:46Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T18:21:46Z","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["area:messages-protobuf"]}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.3","title":"Implement protobuf snapshot visitors and dataset decoders","description":"Implement SchemaSnapshotEncoder, SchemaSnapshotDecoder, DataSnapshotEncoder, and DataSnapshotDecoder in flotsync_messages for the specialized protobuf format, following the existing schema/datamodel snapshot flow end to end.","acceptance_criteria":"1) flotsync_messages provides working protobuf-backed implementations of the schema snapshot visitor and decoder traits. 2) Dataset-level row encoding and lazy row decoding work through InMemoryData encode_data_snapshots and decode_data_snapshots. 3) The implementation uses the specialized snapshot payload codecs rather than per-CRDT bespoke paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.5","title":"Implement direct protobuf conversions for schema operations","description":"Implement direct protobuf message conversions for SchemaOperation\u003cUpdateId\u003e and OperationValue\u003cUpdateId\u003e across the full datamodel operation surface, including history-backed and state-based field variants.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Protobuf conversions cover LatestValueWins, LinearString, LinearList, MonotonicCounter, TotalOrderRegister, and TotalOrderFiniteStateRegister operation variants. 2) LinearString and LinearList insert and delete forms are both represented. 3) The helpers validate or preserve enough structure to roundtrip back into the datamodel operation APIs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:30Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:30Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6","title":"Benchmark a columnar HistorySnapshot format against the current node-oriented encoding","description":"Once the baseline protobuf snapshot encoder/decoder exists, add an alternative HistorySnapshot storage format that stores node metadata columnar-style and packs node payloads into shared value arrays recoverable from the metadata. Benchmark the baseline and alternative formats against each other for encoded size plus encode/decode performance on representative history snapshots, and capture the tradeoffs and recommendation.","acceptance_criteria":"1) An alternative HistorySnapshot representation is prototyped for benchmarking, using columnar node metadata plus shared value storage. 2) Benchmarks compare the baseline and alternative snapshot formats for encoded size and encode/decode performance on representative datasets. 3) Results are recorded with a clear comparison and recommendation on whether to keep, discard, or further iterate on the columnar format.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T07:09:28Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T07:09:28Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6.1","title":"Prototype the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot wire format and codec","description":"Add the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot protobuf messages and a low-level benchmark-only codec path that stores node metadata separately from dense value arrays.","acceptance_criteria":"1) datamodel.proto contains an experimental columnar HistorySnapshot representation with node metadata and a dense PrimitiveArrayValue payload section. 2) flotsync_messages has low-level encode/decode helpers for the experimental format covering LatestValueWins, LinearString, and LinearList. 3) The experimental codec remains isolated from the current public snapshot adapter path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:26Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:26Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6.2","title":"Add correctness fixtures and roundtrip tests for the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot","description":"Add representative history fixtures and correctness coverage for the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot codec, including boundary nodes, tombstones, null payloads, and empty arrays where allowed by schema.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Deterministic fixtures cover LatestValueWins, LinearString, and LinearList history streams with representative edge cases. 2) Experimental-format roundtrip tests verify node equivalence and reconstructed CRDT integrity. 3) Edge cases cover boundary nodes, tombstones, nullable values, and empty array payloads where valid.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:31Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:31Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6.3","title":"Benchmark node-oriented vs experimental columnar HistorySnapshot formats and record a recommendation","description":"Add a benchmark harness comparing the current node-oriented HistorySnapshot format with the experimental columnar alternative for encoded size, encode/decode cost, and reconstruction cost, then record the results and recommendation.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Benchmarks compare current and experimental HistorySnapshot formats for encoded size and encode/decode performance on representative fixtures. 2) The benchmark harness includes medium and large histories plus tombstone-heavy cases. 3) Results are written down with a clear keep/discard/iterate recommendation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:37Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:37Z"}
